Show All Posts to Backend Users: Show All Posts to Backend Users
Display both published and unpublished p: Display both published and unpublished posts on the frontend to backend users
Enable the older version of the markdown: Enable the older version of the markdown editor when using October CMS v2 and above
Use common richtext editor instead of th: Use common richtext editor instead of the Markdown editor
Select a page to open for the Preview bu: Select a page to open for the Preview button
Title: Title
Select categories the blog post belongs: Select categories the blog post belongs to
There are no categories, you should crea: There are no categories, you should create one first!
Name: Name
Return to the blog category list: Return to the blog category list
Displays a list of blog categories on th: Displays a list of blog categories on the page.
Look up the blog category using the supp: Look up the blog category using the supplied slug value. This property is used by the default component partial for marking the currently active category.
Show categories that do not have any pos: Show categories that do not have any posts.
Name of the category page file for the c: Name of the category page file for the category links. This property is used by the default component partial.
Displays a blog post on the page.
Look up the blog post using the supplied: Look up the blog post using the supplied slug value.
Name of the category page file for the c: Name of the category page file for the category links. This property is used by the default component partial.
Displays a list of latest blog posts on: Displays a list of latest blog posts on the page.
This value is used to determine what pag: This value is used to determine what page the user is on.
Enter a category slug or URL parameter t: Enter a category slug or URL parameter to filter the posts by. Leave empty to show all posts.
Invalid format of the posts per page val: Invalid format of the posts per page value
Message to display in the blog post list: Message to display in the blog post list in case if there are no posts. This property is used by the default component partial.
Attribute on which the posts should be o: Attribute on which the posts should be ordered
Name of the category page file for the \: Name of the category page file for the "Posted into" category links. This property is used by the default component partial.
Name of the blog post page file for the: Name of the blog post page file for the "Learn more" links. This property is used by the default component partial.
Enter ID/URL or variable with post ID/UR: Enter ID/URL or variable with post ID/URL you want to exclude. You may use a comma-separated list to specify multiple posts.
Post exceptions must be a single slug or: Post exceptions must be a single slug or ID, or a comma-separated list of slugs and IDs
Enter a comma-separated list of category: Enter a comma-separated list of category slugs or variable with such a list of categories you want to exclude
Category exceptions must be a single cat: Category exceptions must be a single category slug, or a comma-separated list of slugs
Name of the main blog page file for gene: Name of the main blog page file for generating links. This property is used by the default component partial.
Generates an RSS feed containing posts f: Generates an RSS feed containing posts from the blog.
Check this box to update posts that have: Check this box to update posts that have exactly the same ID, title or slug.
Create categories specified in the impor: Create categories specified in the import file
You should match the Categories column t: You should match the Categories column to use this feature, otherwise select the default categories to use from the items below.
Select the categories that imported post: Select the categories that imported posts will belong to (optional).
The import will try to use an existing a: The import will try to use an existing author if you match the Author Email column, otherwise the author specified above is used.
